title: Utilities Billing and Payment System Evaluation: 2026 Billing and Payment Platform Selection Guide
description: Professional evaluation of 2026 utilities billing and payment systems, covering billing engines, payment platforms, electronic bills, and prepaid management. Deep analysis of Oracle Billing, Vertex, Cayenta, and OpenEdge utilities billing solution features and user experience. Learn more about features and price comparisons to find the best solution! Professional evaluation helps you make decisions!
content:
Utilities Billing and Payment System Evaluation: 2026 Billing and Payment Platform Selection Guide

Complexity of Utilities Billing Management and User Expectations

Electricity, water, and gas utilities billing management involves complex billing rules, large-scale bill generation, multi-channel payments, and user payment experience. In 2026, the global utilities billing management software market size reaches $2.5 billion, growing 10% annually. Traditional billing systems have poor billing flexibility, long bill generation cycles, single payment channels, and poor user experience. Modern billing management systems support complex rate configuration, real-time bill generation, multi-channel payments, electronic bill push notifications, and prepaid management, improving billing accuracy, shortening bill cycles, and improving payment experience.

Tiered pricing, time-of-use billing, multi-service combination billing, and renewable energy rates create billing engine flexibility requirements. Users expect real-time viewing of usage, electronic bill push notifications, and convenient multi-channel payments. Billing management system selection requires consideration of billing engine capabilities, bill generation efficiency, payment channel integration, electronic bill features, prepaid support, and user experience.

In-depth Evaluation: Mainstream Utilities Billing and Payment Systems

1. Oracle Utilities Customer Care and Billing

Oracle Utilities CCB is a utilities billing benchmark, with billing engine supporting most complex rate scenarios, suitable for large utilities.

Core Features:

  • Billing engine: Configurable rate rules thousands of types, supporting tiered pricing, time-of-use billing, and combination billing
  • Bill generation: Large-scale bill batch generation and printing
  • Payment management: Bank deduction, online payment, counter payment, mobile payment
  • Electronic bills: Email push notification electronic bills
  • Prepaid: Prepaid customer real-time deduction management
  • Bill adjustment: Bill adjustment and refund processing

Technical Features: Billing engine most complex scenario support, suitable for renewable energy rates and multi-service combination billing.

Price Range: Enterprise-level license 20-100 thousand dollars.

2. Vertex Billing Platform

Vertex billing platform focuses on billing engine professionalism, with highly configurable rate rule engine, suitable for complex billing scenarios.

Core Features:

  • Billing engine: Rate rule engine highly configurable
  • Bill generation: Bill generation and export
  • Payment management: Payment data integration with financial system
  • Billing analysis: Billing data analysis
  • Rate management: Rate configuration and version management
  • Billing validation: Billing accuracy validation

Price Range: Billing engine license 5-20 thousand dollars.

3. Cayenta Billing System

Cayenta billing system focuses on utilities billing and customer management, suitable for medium-sized utilities.

Core Features:

  • Billing engine: Supports tiered pricing and time-of-use billing
  • Bill generation: Bill batch generation
  • Payment management: Multi-channel payments
  • Customer management: Customer archives and service management
  • Electronic bills: Electronic bill push notifications
  • Report generation: Billing and revenue reports

Price Range: Software license 5-20 thousand dollars.

4. OpenEdge Payment Platform for Utilities

OpenEdge is a payment platform, with utilities version focusing on payment channel integration and payment processing.

Core Features:

  • Payment channels: Bank deduction, credit card, mobile payment integration
  • Payment processing: Payment transaction processing and settlement
  • Mobile payment: Mobile APP payment
  • Automatic payment: Automatic deduction and reminders
  • Payment security: Payment data security and compliance
  • Payment analysis: Payment data analysis

Price Range: Payment platform billed by transaction volume, monthly fee 1-5 thousand dollars.

5. Hansen Billing Express

Hansen Billing Express billing system suitable for medium-sized utilities, integrated billing and customer management.

Core Features:

  • Billing engine: Medium complexity rate support
  • Bill generation: Bill batch generation
  • Payment management: Multi-channel payments
  • Customer management: Customer archives management
  • Electronic bills: Electronic bill push notifications
  • Prepaid: Prepaid management

Price Range: Software license 5-15 thousand dollars.

6. FICS Billing and Payment

FICS billing system focuses on small and medium utilities, cost-effective.

Core Features:

  • Billing engine: Basic to medium complexity rates
  • Bill generation: Bill generation
  • Payment management: Bank deduction, online payment
  • Electronic bills: Electronic bill push notifications
  • Customer portal: Customers view bills and make payments
  • Report generation: Billing reports

Price Range: Software license 2-10 thousand dollars.

7. NorthStar Utility Billing

NorthStar billing system suitable for North American small and medium municipal utilities.

Core Features:

  • Billing engine: Basic rate support
  • Bill generation: Bill generation
  • Payment management: Multi-channel payments
  • Customer portal: CitizenServe self-service payment
  • Electronic bills: Electronic bill push notifications
  • Mobile payment: Mobile APP payment

Price Range: SaaS subscription annual fee 1-5 thousand dollars.

8. Paymentus Utility Payment Platform

Paymentus is a utilities payment platform, focusing on multi-channel payments and user experience.

Core Features:

  • Payment channels: Bank deduction, credit card, electronic wallet integration
  • Mobile payment: Mobile APP and electronic wallet payment
  • Automatic payment: Automatic deduction and reminders
  • Payment security: Payment security and compliance
  • User experience: Payment process optimization
  • Payment analysis: Payment data analysis

Price Range: Payment platform billed by transaction volume.

9. Stripe Billing for Utilities

Stripe billing platform focuses on subscription billing scenarios, suitable for prepaid utilities customers.

Core Features:

  • Subscription billing: Subscription mode billing management
  • Payment processing: Stripe payment gateway
  • Automatic payment: Automatic deduction
  • Mobile payment: Mobile payment support
  • Payment security: Stripe payment security
  • Data analysis: Billing data analysis

Price Range: Billed by transaction volume, suitable for prepaid scenarios.

10. Killbill Open Source Billing

Killbill open source billing platform, suitable for enterprises with strong technical capabilities pursuing low costs.

Core Features:

  • Billing engine: Open source billing engine customizable
  • Subscription billing: Subscription mode billing
  • Payment integration: Payment gateway integration
  • Billing analysis: Basic analysis
  • Open source customization: Secondary development extension

Price Range: Open source free, customization development requires investment.

Selection Strategy and Scenario Matching

Scenario 1: Complex Billing Scenarios

Tiered pricing, time-of-use billing, renewable energy rates, and multi-service combination billing. Oracle CCB billing engine supports most complex scenarios.

Scenario 2: Large-scale Bill Generation

Million customers monthly bill batch generation. Oracle CCB, Cayenta, and Hansen have high bill batch generation efficiency.

Scenario 3: Payment Channel Integration

Bank deduction, credit card, mobile payment, and electronic wallet multi-channel integration. OpenEdge and Paymentus lead payment platforms.

Scenario 4: Electronic Bill Push Notifications

Email push notification electronic bills reducing paper bill costs. Oracle, Cayenta, Hansen, and FICS have comprehensive electronic bill features.

Scenario 5: Prepaid Management

Prepaid customer real-time deduction and balance reminders. Oracle CCB and Hansen have comprehensive prepaid management.

Scenario 6: User Experience Priority

Mobile payment, automatic deduction, and payment reminders improving experience. Paymentus, NorthStar, and OpenEdge lead user experience.

Scenario 7: Small Medium Enterprise Low Cost

Limited budget and basic billing requirements. FICS, NorthStar, and Killbill low cost solutions.

Scenario 8: Subscription Billing Mode

Subscription mode billing and automatic deduction. Stripe Billing and Killbill specialize in subscription billing.

2026 Utilities Billing Development Trends

Trend 1: Real-time Billing and Prepaid

Smart meter adoption driving real-time billing, prepaid customer real-time deduction reducing arrears risk. Oracle CCB PrePay module and prepaid solutions growing.

Trend 2: Mobile Payment Adoption

Users paying via mobile APP, mobile payment becoming mainstream channel. Paymentus, NorthStar, and OpenEdge lead mobile payment.

Trend 3: Electronic Bills Replacing Paper

Electronic bills reducing paper bill costs and environmental pressure. Mainstream billing systems all support electronic bill push notifications.

Trend 4: Automatic Deduction and Reminders

Automatic deduction avoiding users forgetting payments, balance reminders preventing service interruption. Paymentus and Stripe have comprehensive automatic deduction features.

Trend 5: Payment Experience Optimization

Payment process simplification, one-click payment, and multiple payment methods improving user experience. Paymentus and NorthStar lead payment experience.

Implementation Suggestions and Risk Alerts

Billing Rules Validation: Billing engine requires detailed testing of rate configuration capabilities, validating complex billing scenario accuracy.

Payment Channel Integration: Validate payment platform integration with local banks and payment channels.

Bill Accuracy: Bill generation requires validating metering data integration accuracy, avoiding billing errors.

Prepaid Security: Prepaid real-time deduction requires validating balance calculation accuracy, avoiding service interruption.

Payment Compliance: Payment processing must comply with PCI-DSS and other payment security standards.

Summary

In 2026, the utilities billing and payment system market forms seven categories: complex billing engine, professional billing platform, medium-sized billing system, payment platform, small medium enterprise, subscription billing, and open source customization. For complex billing selection, choose Oracle CCB; for professional billing engine, use Vertex; for medium-sized billing, choose Cayenta and Hansen; for payment platform, use OpenEdge and Paymentus; for small medium enterprises, use FICS and NorthStar; for subscription billing, choose Stripe Billing; for open source solution, use Killbill. Selection should focus on billing engine capabilities as core, matching customer scale and payment experience requirements, gradually introducing real-time billing and mobile payment.
author: UtilitiesBilling Expert Team
